codeReview.summary: The worker correctly identified that most SEO metadata and accessibility requirements were already fulfilled by prior features. The actual diff is small (8 files, 24 insertions, 7 deletions) because the main work was auditing the codebase and filling in the remaining gaps: (1) Added full metadata + OpenGraph to the homepage which had none, (2) Added OpenGraph tags to the shop page which was missing them, (3) Added flex-1 class to 6 content components for consistent footer positioning (ensuring footer stays at bottom on short pages), (4) Changed PDPContent root from <div> to semantic <main> element. The worker verified all pages have proper metadata, all images use Next.js Image with descriptive alt text, ARIA labels are present on 35+ interactive elements, focus states are globally defined, heading hierarchy is correct (one h1 per page), and build/typecheck/lint all pass clean. The responsive testing was limited by agent-browser's fixed viewport (1280x720), so the worker verified responsive classes rather than testing at all 4 specified widths.

issues.description: Root layout metadata lacks OpenGraph tags. While all individual pages define their own OG tags, the root layout serves as a fallback for any page that might not define its own metadata (e.g., the ui-test page). Adding openGraph to root layout would provide a safety net.

OrderConfigurator has two h1 elements (lines 1139 and 1171), though they are conditionally rendered (order confirmed vs main page). While functionally correct since only one renders at a time, this pattern could trip up static HTML auditing tools. The worker correctly identified and documented this in the handoff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature description requested testing at 375px, 768px, 1024px, and 1440px widths, but AGENTS.md notes that agent-browser always opens at 1280x720 and does not support changing viewport width. The convention for mobile verification is CSS class review. This limitation meant the worker could not actually test at all 4 breakpoints visually — only via responsive class inspection. AGENTS.md already documents this limitation clearly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md 'agent-browser viewport limitation' section. Worker handoff confirms verification was done via grep for responsive classes and agent-browser at default width.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature description asked to 'add loading=lazy where appropriate', but Next.js Image component defaults to lazy loading automatically. The worker correctly omitted explicit loading props. This implicit behavior is not documented in AGENTS.md but is standard Next.js knowledge — no action needed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: No 'loading' prop found in any Image component across src/. Next.js docs confirm Image defaults to loading='lazy'.

summary: PASS. The responsive-polish-and-seo feature is essentially an audit/gap-fill pass. The worker correctly identified that most requirements (SEO metadata, accessibility, Image usage, build cleanliness) were already satisfied by prior features. The actual code changes are minimal but appropriate: adding missing metadata to the homepage, adding OG tags to the shop page, fixing footer positioning with flex-1, and improving PDP semantic HTML. All verification checks (build, typecheck, lint) pass. The only non-blocking issue is the root layout missing OpenGraph tags as a fallback. The responsive testing was inherently limited by the agent-browser constraint, but the worker compensated by auditing responsive CSS classes.
